details: Find nearest center at uidai.gov.in or on Aadhaar app

documents_required: Proof of Identity (POI): Passport/PAN card/Voter ID/Driving Licence. Proof of Address (POA): Passport/Bank Statement/Utility Bill/Ration Card. Proof of Date of Birth (DOB): Birth Certificate/Passport/PAN card
details: Form is available free at enrollment centre
Fingerprints and iris scan will be taken
Contains 14-digit Enrollment ID to track status
Aadhaar sent by post or download from uidai.gov.in using enrollment ID
Online: voterportal.eci.gov.in or offline at BLO/ERO office

documents_required: Age proof (DOB certificate/Passport/PAN/Driving Licence/Aadhaar). Address proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Bank Passbook/Utility Bill). Passport size photograph (2 copies)
details: Must be 18 years or above as on January 1 of the qualifying year
Submit at Electoral Registration Officer or upload online
Booth Level Officer visits for physical verification
EPIC card sent by post or download e-EPIC from voterportal.eci.gov.in
Visit parivahan.gov.in and select your state RTO

documents_required: Age proof (DOB certificate/Aadhaar/Passport). Address proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Utility Bill). Passport size photographs (3 copies). Medical certificate Form 1A for commercial licence
details: Minimum age 16 for gearless vehicles 18 for geared vehicles
Fee paid online at Parivahan portal

documents_required: Learner licence application receipt and ID proof
details: Computer-based test of 15 questions at RTO. Need to score at least 9 correct
Valid for 6 months. Must practice for minimum 30 days before applying for permanent licence

step: 1. Apply after 30 days of learner licence
details: Apply online at parivahan.gov.in or visit RTO

documents_required: Learner Licence. Passport size photographs (3). Address and age proof
details: Book slot at least 30 days after learner licence issue date

documents_required: Learner Licence and all original documents
details: Practical driving test conducted at RTO by licensing officer
DL sent by post to registered address or download DigiLocker
Go to police station having jurisdiction over the area where offence occurred

step: 2. Give complaint orally or in writing
documents_required: Written complaint signed by complainant. Identity proof of complainant (Aadhaar/Voter ID)
details: Police must register FIR for cognizable offences. Cannot refuse to register FIR
FIR assigned a unique number. Copy must be given to complainant FREE of charge
Section 154(2) CrPC mandates free copy of FIR to complainant. Police cannot deny this

step: 5. If police refuse to register FIR
documents_required: Written complaint to Superintendent of Police (SP). Or file complaint before Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC
details: SP must either investigate or order registration. Magistrate can direct police to register FIR
Many states allow online FIR for certain offences like theft/loss of documents at respective state police portal

step: 1. Hire a lawyer or apply yourself
details: Bail can be applied by accused or their lawyer at appropriate court

documents_required: For bailable offences: Application to police officer or Magistrate. For non-bailable offences: Application to Sessions Court or High Court. Anticipatory bail: Application to Sessions Court or High Court before arrest
details: Bailable offences: Bail is a right. Non-bailable: Bail is discretionary

documents_required: Bail application drafted by lawyer. FIR copy. Arrest memo. Identity and address proof of accused. Surety documents if cash bail. Character certificate if needed
details: Court considers nature of offence criminal history flight risk and likelihood of tampering evidence

documents_required: Surety: Person who guarantees accused will appear in court. Property documents or cash deposit may be required. Personal bond (PR bond) for minor offences
details: Amount of bail bond fixed by court based on gravity of offence
Accused released on furnishing bail bond. Must comply with all bail conditions including appearing for all hearings
Identify which government department/ministry has the information you need

documents_required: RTI application addressed to Public Information Officer (PIO). Clearly mention information required. No reason needs to be given for seeking information. Application in English Hindi or local official language
details: Keep application simple and specific. Vague applications may be rejected

documents_required: Rs 10 by cash/demand draft/postal order/online. BPL card holders are exempt from fee. No fee for life and liberty related information
details: Additional fee of Rs 2 per page for documents provided

documents_required: RTI application with proof of fee payment
details: Submit to PIO of concerned department by post in person or online at rtionline.gov.in for central government
PIO must provide information within 30 days. For life and liberty matters within 48 hours

documents_required: First Appeal application to First Appellate Authority (FAA). Copy of original RTI application. Copy of reply if any received
details: If PIO does not respond within 30 days or response is unsatisfactory file first appeal within 30 days of deadline

documents_required: Second Appeal application to Central/State Information Commission. Copies of RTI application first appeal and replies
details: File within 90 days of first appeal order. CIC/SIC can impose penalty of Rs 250 per day up to Rs 25000 on PIO for delay

documents_required: Title deed/Sale deed. Encumbrance certificate from Sub-Registrar office. Property tax receipts. Approved building plan. NOC from society/builder if applicable
details: Check for any existing loans disputes or encumbrances on property
Sale deed drafted by lawyer mentioning property details buyer seller consideration amount and terms

documents_required: Sale deed. Property valuation certificate. Identity proofs of buyer and seller
details: Stamp duty paid online or at bank. Karnataka: 5% Delhi: 6% Maharashtra: 5-6% UP: 7%

documents_required: Original sale deed on stamp paper. Identity proof of buyer seller and two witnesses (Aadhaar/PAN). Passport size photographs of buyer and seller. Property documents. Stamp duty payment receipt. Registration fee payment receipt
fees: 1% of property value (max Rs 30000 in most states)
details: Both buyer and seller must be present with two witnesses. Biometric verification done
Registered document given back same day. Original title deed kept on record
Apply for mutation at municipal office to update property records in new owners name

documents_required: Bride minimum age 18 years. Groom minimum age 21 years. Both must be unmarried or legally divorced/widowed
details: Hindu Marriage Act for Hindus. Special Marriage Act for inter-religion marriages. Muslim personal law for Muslims
Online at state government portal or offline at Sub-Divisional Magistrate SDM office or municipal office

documents_required: Marriage application form. Age proof of bride and groom (Birth certificate/Passport/SSC marksheet). Address proof of bride and groom (Aadhaar/Passport). Identity proof (Aadhaar/PAN/Passport). Marriage photograph. Wedding invitation card. Affidavit of marital status. Two passport photos each of bride and groom. Marriage solemnization proof (pandit certificate/nikahnama/church certificate)
details: Under Hindu Marriage Act registration must be done within 30 days of marriage in some states

documents_required: Two witnesses with identity and address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID). Witnesses must have attended the marriage
details: Under Special Marriage Act 30-day notice period required and marriage officer publishes notice
Documents verified by registrar. Appointment given for registration
Marriage certificate issued on the day of registration. Valid legal document for all purposes
Birth must be reported to municipal corporation/gram panchayat within 21 days of birth

documents_required: Hospital discharge summary/birth proof. Parents Aadhaar card. Parents marriage certificate. Informants identity proof
fees: Free if within 21 days. Rs 50-100 after that
details: Apply at local municipal corporation urban local body or gram panchayat

documents_required: If more than 21 days: Affidavit from parents. If more than 1 year: Order from First Class Magistrate required. Hospital records. Vaccination records
fees: Rs 50-500 + court fees for late registration
details: Magistrate order required for births not registered within 1 year
Certificate issued by Registrar of Births and Deaths. Available online in many states

documents_required: Death should be reported by head of household or hospital/jail/plantation where death occurred
details: Report to local municipal corporation/gram panchayat within 21 days

documents_required: Hospital death summary or post-mortem report. Deceased persons Aadhaar card. Informants identity proof. Address proof
details: Apply at municipal corporation or gram panchayat office
Required for insurance claims property transfer pension and other legal purposes

documents_required: Insurance claims. Bank account closure. Property inheritance. Pension/gratuity claims. Cancel Aadhaar and Voter ID of deceased
details: Must inform Aadhaar (UIDAI) Voter registration authority bank and other institutions
